step_by_step_thinking:
Yes, I agree with the selection of option C - capillaries as having the minimum blood pressure. This agreement stems primarily from the fact that capillaries offer reduced vascular resistance due to their thin-walled structure, allowing them to maintain relatively low internal pressures despite significant blood flow demands across such networks. Additionally, their enormous surface area supports an increased rate of fluid exchange without raising localized intravascular stress levels excessively. Therefore, my position aligns fully with the provided evidence emphasizing both anatomical features and functional roles characteristic of capillaries.
